question: Why did the Space Shuttle Challenger explode?
text:
The Space Shuttle Challenger exploded because the O-ring seal in the right solid rocket booster failed, allowing hot gases to escape and ignite the external fuel tank. The O-rings failed due to the cold temperature on the day of the accident. The rubber O-rings became stiff and failed to seal properly, which led to the catastrophic failure. The failure of the O-ring seal was the primary cause of the explosion. (Source: NASA) [1]

References:
[1] NASA. (n.d.). Space Shuttle Challenger Accident.
